context: Checks whether the final artifact follows the apply-phoenix-liveview-conventions instructions from the published Elixir Phoenix Skills plugin.

checklist.name: instruction-1
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: Use this skill when writing new LiveView modules or modifying existing LiveView code to ensure consistent, idiomatic Phoenix patterns.

checklist.name: instruction-2
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Always use `@impl true`** before every callback (mount, handle_event, handle_info, handle_params, render)

checklist.name: instruction-3
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Assign errors to socket, don't raise** — use `put_flash` and changeset assigns for error states

checklist.name: instruction-4
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Use function components (def, not defp)** for reusable HEEx markup — export via `~H"""` sigil

checklist.name: instruction-5
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Use `with` for multi-step error handling** in event handlers instead of nested case

checklist.name: instruction-6
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Never query the database directly from a LiveView** — call context functions instead

checklist.name: instruction-7
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Always initialize assigns to safe defaults in Phase 1** so the static HTML never raises a `KeyError` before WebSocket connects.

checklist.name: instruction-8
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Checkpoint:** Static HTML must render without `KeyError` before WebSocket connects.
